title: Big-WORD Motions
intro: Lowercase `w b e` stop at every punctuation boundary. Their uppercase cousins `W B E` treat any run of non-blank characters as one WORD — so `foo.bar` is a single WORD. Use them to skip past punctuation-heavy code fast.

exercises.instruction: Jump to the start of `baz-qux` with one press of `W` (it skips the whole `foo.bar` at once).
hints: `W` moves to the next whitespace-separated WORD, ignoring the `.` and `-`.
Two presses of `W` skip past both `foo.bar` and `baz-qux`.

exercises.instruction: Jump to the END of the first WORD (`foo.bar`, the `r`) with `E`.
hints: `E` lands on the last character of the current/next WORD.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on the last WORD. Jump back to the start of `baz-qux` with `B`.
hints: `B` jumps back one WORD, treating punctuation as part of the WORD.

exercises.instruction: Jump to `now` with two presses of `W` — the whole URL counts as one WORD.
hints: Lowercase `w` would stop at every `/` and `.` — `W` skips the URL in one hop.

exercises.instruction: The cursor is on `now`. Jump back onto the START of the URL with `B`.
hints: `B` treats the entire URL as a single WORD.

exercises.instruction: Land on the LAST character of the URL by pressing `E` twice.
hints: The first `E` reaches the end of `fetch`; the second reaches the end of the URL.
